findings.description: The PDF has a single unified table with location details and call-in details side by side in the header area, while the HTML separates these into a distinct 'Location Details' / 'Call-In Details' table and then a separate agenda table below, changing the visual structure.
findings.evidence: PDF: Location and call-in info are within the same bordered table as the rest of the agenda header. HTML: They are in a separate table with 'Location Details' and 'Call-In Details' column headers, followed by a separate 'Time' / 'Agenda Item' table.

findings.description: The PDF agenda is presented as a single table with time slots and descriptions in a unified format. The HTML splits this into two separate tables (location/call-in details table and agenda items table), but the data content and structure are preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF: One large table encompassing everything. HTML: Two tables but same data preserved.

findings.description: Some italic formatting from the PDF is not preserved in the HTML for presenter names and titles.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Benita Purcell and Mark Fuhrmann' is in italics, 'Consent Agenda' is in italics, presenter titles like 'JD, Acting Assistant Director for Systems Performance' are in italics. HTML: These appear in regular (non-italic) text.

findings.description: The main title in the PDF is centered and bold within the table, while the HTML renders it as a large heading at the top of the page outside any table structure.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'State Consumer & Family Advisory Committee AGENDA* July 10, 2019 | Time: 9:00 am – 3:00 pm' centered in table header. HTML: Title as a large heading, 'AGENDA*' as a separate bold line, date as smaller underlined text.

findings.description: The underlined formatting on 'CALL-IN #' and 'ACCESS CODE' in the PDF header is preserved in the HTML as bold text, maintaining emphasis but through different styling.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'CALL-IN #:' and 'ACCESS CODE:' are underlined and bold. HTML: They appear bold without underline.

findings.description: HTML adds column headers 'Location Details' and 'Call-In Details' for the location/call-in table, and 'Time' and 'Agenda Item' for the agenda table. These headers do not appear in the PDF but serve as structural aids.
findings.evidence: PDF has no explicit column headers for the location/call-in info block or the agenda table. HTML introduces <th>Location Details</th>, <th>Call-In Details</th>, <th>Time</th>, <th>Agenda Item</th>.

findings.description: The contact person info is placed in the location/call-in table as a separate row spanning incorrectly. In the PDF, 'Kate Barrow [Contact Person] (919) 621-1116' appears aligned with the call-in details section, but in the HTML it's placed in the left column of a new row with an empty right cell.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Kate Barrow contact info aligned right/under call-in details. HTML places it in <td> under Location Details column.

findings.description: The HTML uses h2 for 'AGENDA*' which is acceptable, but the document title 'State Consumer & Family Advisory Committee' is set as h1 and 'AGENDA*' as h2, which is a reasonable representation of the document structure.
findings.evidence:
PDF title block: 'State Consumer & Family Advisory Committee AGENDA*'. HTML:

State Consumer & Family Advisory Committee

AGENDA*

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> tags (two levels), which is a structural duplication but does not affect content fidelity.
findings.evidence: HTML contains <main> inside <body> which contains another <main> inside it.
